title: v0.6.0
summary: May expanded Consuelo's internal operating system: workspace agents gained safer facades, richer trace visibility, search, and browser tooling while Consuelo OS, the design wiki, and dialer reliability moved forward together.

weeks.label: Week of May 1 through May 8
weeks.summary: The month opened with dialer reliability fixes around empty queues and GraphQL call starts, plus stronger workspace tooling for reviews, Sentry, Linear, task sessions, and design-system work.

sections.title: Dialer
items: Prevented invalid Current Contact displays for empty queues and completed empty queue starts when no callable items are available.
Unblocked autostart flows when transient Twilio status errors or zero-member queues would otherwise stop calling.
Added and validated GraphQL call-start scenarios, then hardened the live GraphQL call lifecycle and Storybook test imports.
Expanded the dialer scenario harness so queue and call-start regressions can be reproduced locally.

sections.title: Workspace Agents
items: Fixed review command handling, made filesystem patches safe for multiline edits, and made verify flows branch-explicit.
Added typed Sentry facade commands, Linear facade commands, tmux-backed workspace calls, and time beacons in tool result envelopes.
Cleaned task-session followups and removed steering cache behavior that could leave agents with stale context.

sections.title: Design
items: Created the Consuelo design-system guide and added the upstream facade package to the Bun workspace tooling.
Added workflow-start commands, tools-dev JSON parsing, and restored Open Design artifact helpers.

sections.title: Docs and Steering
items: Established Markdown as the default writing format and added syntax, fence-integrity, and steering usage guidance.
Clarified agent handoff, coding standards, and dialer-specific operating instructions.

weeks.label: Week of May 8 through May 15
weeks.summary: Workspace tooling became safer and more research-oriented, Consuelo OS received its first runtime scaffold, and the design wiki moved toward a durable reader/archive surface.

sections.title: Workspace Agents
items: Added server safety guardrails, scoped audits to task sessions, supported object-wrapped batch inputs, and cleaned up task tmux sessions.
Improved LangSmith trace summaries, research ingest packets, automatic research bundle saving, and JSON output for context commands.
Added browser facade primitives, responsive browser flags, target-aware decision reads, tree-sitter target metadata, file-backed writes, and a context trace SQLite store.
Made Consuelo Design headless by default and selected extracted content during research ingest.

sections.title: OS
items: Scaffolded the Consuelo OS runtime and documentation, then aligned OS manifests with the script runtime.
Started the Bun runtime switch planning for Consuelo OS.

sections.title: Design Wiki
items: Added design publish Tailscale links, digital e-guide templates, and the first design wiki reader shell.
Made the design archive durable and smoothed the reader shell experience.
Redesigned the Consuelo wiki index and added resume-reading and section-completion rail interactions.

sections.title: Dialer
items: Switched call starts to the metadata client path and addressed review findings around dialer tests.

weeks.label: Week of May 15 through May 22
weeks.summary: Tracing moved to Langfuse, code execution became a first-class workspace namespace, OS documentation deepened, and queue calling picked up safer runtime identifiers.

sections.title: Workspace Agents
items: Switched tracing to Langfuse and wired token usage metadata into trace reporting.
Preferred Graphite PR links and preserved those links across task PR flows.
Added native code.run support, namespace helpers for code execution, and routing that keeps code execution inside workspace tools.
Kept the workspace server responsive during long-running tool calls and hardened Bun orchestration for code execution.
Namespaced task metadata by task path so concurrent task sessions do not overwrite each other.

sections.title: OS
items: Replaced shallow OS docs with real guides, added the OS navigation skeleton, and renamed runbooks into skills.
Persisted skill artifacts and added the local install wizard plus CLI front door.

sections.title: Dialer
items: Sent explicit runtime queue identifiers for predictive calls and fixed queue eligibility behavior.
Confirmed metadata route validation around predictive queue execution.

sections.title: Auth
items: Bound Redis session and metadata cache failures so authentication survives cache-side outages more predictably.

sections.title: Steering
items: Updated agent guidance around Graphite workflows and removed stale task-pin language from steering.

weeks.label: Week of May 22 through May 29
weeks.summary: The largest May push added typed GitHub and diff tooling, deeper trace observability, OS capability and skill foundations, stronger design-wiki publishing, and a safer dialer queue-target model.

sections.title: Workspace Agents
items: Added typed GitHub and git-diff facades, registered the diff schema, and included untracked files in diff output.
Improved task-session resolution from MCP calls, moved tmux payloads to temporary scripts, and kept facade command formatting lossless.
Added operator trace analytics, trace watch, trace error reports, compact success rows, Eastern-time rendering, nested operation details, token counts, verify reasons, and batch child trace names.
Made review runs resumable, hardened verify publish gates, compacted review and verify output, seeded the test-selection registry, and improved explore relevance ranking.
Added neutral command aliases, tool-not-found recovery, 2560-dimensional embedding benchmarks, worker providers, and clearer explore result interpretation.

sections.title: OS
items: Added the capability registry and local guardrails for OS work.
Added OS skills for the Consuelo Design landing page, design orchestration, workspace snapshots, app-file cloud artifacts, task workflow, and skills registry generation.
Removed misplaced worker providers from the OS stream and kept the OS skill substrate aligned with workspace tooling.

sections.title: Design Wiki
items: Restored design archive publishing and repaired artifact routing, Pagefind publishing, archive ordering, and stale-page refresh behavior.
Added Pagefind search to the design wiki and republished the archive after routing repairs.
Investigated Cloudflare sharing for wiki publishing architecture.

sections.title: Dialer
items: Resolved queue targets from person records and added fallbacks for person phone numbers and queue targets.
Removed invalid target SQL fields and hydrated list queues by person identifier.
Aligned sidebar keyboard shortcut hints and right-aligned the hint treatment.

sections.title: Steering
items: Reworked communication style, workspace tool guidance, timeout budgets, and steering structure for clearer agent execution.

weeks.label: Week of May 29 through Jun 5
weeks.summary: May closed with better tool discovery, a compact worker runtime, worker event ingestion, and a richer canonical wiki reader that connects the design surface back to the repo root.

sections.title: Workspace Agents
items: Extracted the worker runtime, added the Pi provider, compacted worker output, and stabilized compact worker parsing.
Added tools.search, tightened its ranking, upgraded tool discovery, and addressed review feedback around the search surface.
Ingested worker events into the trace database and removed temporary worker trace helper files.

sections.title: Design Wiki
items: Refreshed the design wiki theme and repaired archive routing.
Added the canonical wiki reader renderer, exposed wiki rendering at the repo root, and added rich reader section components.

sections.title: OS
items: Synced the workspace development tooling substrate into OS.

sections.title: Steering
items: Added tool-surface recovery guidance and summarized the explore ranker fixes for future agents.

title: v0.5.0
summary: April turned the dialer into a more reliable queue-driven calling system while expanding workspace-agent tooling, docs, CSV and list imports, file surfaces, and website polish.

weeks.label: Week of Mar 27 through Apr 3
weeks.summary: The first April backlog closed the dialer redesign and Consuelo Docs relaunch, with predictive dialing, phone-number caching, and website/blog fixes landing together.

sections.title: Dialer
items: Redesigned the dialer home page as a single guided setup form with source-mode toggles, line-count controls, and cleaner start actions.
Defaulted queue starts to predictive dialing and added Redis-backed phone-number caching with instant local paint after mutations.
Stabilized the setup flow by fixing Lingui runtime crashes, Twilio status imports, missing icons, empty-state flashes, and story/type regressions.
Moved CSV upload into the page header, persisted microphone permission state, and cleaned up the Ask AI and support-modal release paths.

sections.title: Docs
items: Renamed and rebranded the docs as Consuelo Docs with updated sparkle logos, favicon assets, SEO metadata, and a clearer introduction.
Rebuilt the docs sidebar into collapsible Railway-style groups and restored HighLevel, CLI, Chrome Extension, Dialer, Data Model, and GraphQL placement.
Published a complete Dialer documentation rewrite with 18 capability and how-to pages.

sections.title: Website
items: Fixed social links, RSS feed location, the All Posts button, launch overview CTA arrow alignment, and header/back-button type errors.
Cleaned up the help dropdown, moved the old tags route out of the blog flow, and preserved the public blog navigation behavior.

sections.title: Auth and Navigation
items: Improved CLI authentication with Google SSO support.
Registered the Home route, fixed mobile navigation during upgrade flows, and polished the sidebar support hub interactions.

weeks.label: Week of Apr 3 through Apr 10
weeks.summary: Queues, CSV list import, and authenticated API surfaces became usable end to end while the public website received analytics, SEO, and favicon polish.

sections.title: Dialer and Queues
items: Added queue infrastructure for eligibility checks, cadence suppression, retry policy, and parallel dialing strategy support.
Wired list queues into the voice flow and normalized queue payloads, retry metadata, export identifiers, and workspace checks.
Expanded dialer unit coverage and fixed queue creation defaults, call initiation, Twilio signature typing, and disposition updates.

sections.title: API and Security
items: Scaffolded NestJS controllers for calls, queues, and parallel dialing under the v1 API.
Added workspace-scoped API guards and hardened encryption, database constraints, transaction handling, and Twilio request validation.
Fixed API type errors across queue creation, phone initiation, token module wiring, and TwiML response handling.

sections.title: Lists and CSV Import
items: Added CSV import for list members from the home page, including list creation, contact-count updates, and listMember relation support.
Fixed import hook closures, spreadsheet context wiring, localized default names, and i18n loading for translated CSV flows.

sections.title: Navigation and Docs
items: Tracked the last visited page path for Home support and defaulted navigation safely when no previous page exists.
Fixed plan-pill visibility, help dropdown styling/imports, docs translations, and sidebar label capitalization.

sections.title: Website
items: Added PostHog analytics, email tracking, and a glass-style cookie banner.
Overhauled SEO with apex canonicals, shared metadata, robots and llms text, schema markup, tighter favicons, and the Geist Mono font.

weeks.label: Week of Apr 10 through Apr 17
weeks.summary: Dialer intelligence deepened with local presence, Bayesian retry strategy, and Pi-native analysis while Files, CSV matching, keyboard shortcuts, and sync commands landed.

sections.title: Dialer
items: Added notification sounds and custom ringback behavior while suppressing duplicate Twilio audio.
Added Bayesian posterior strategy types, Thompson sampling, hazard-based retry timing, optimal stopping thresholds, and telemetry-backed posterior updates.
Added learned cadence policy, double-dial retry, Whittle-index queue prioritization, and a People tab with Called and Up Next sections.
Fixed call-result duplication, route prefixes, workspace strategy wiring, local presence, missing workspace configs, queue N+1 queries, and build type regressions.

sections.title: CSV Mapping
items: Added AI-powered CSV column matching through the Pi agent skill executor with an AI-first path and Fuse fallback.
Registered authenticated CSV mapping API routes, fixed multi-tenant CORS and request shapes, accepted both columns and headers, and detected the real header row from raw CSV data.
Guarded CSV import default names and defaulted imported lists to sequential names.

sections.title: Files and Knowledge Base
items: Added a Files keyboard shortcut, file preview widget, attachment record-page layout, and server-signed file URLs.
Fixed knowledge-base guard imports and file-storage dependency injection crashes, then documented the File System surface.

sections.title: Workspace
items: Added a workspace:sync-standard command with detailed sync and migration logging.
Replaced the internal-brain package with openworkspace and removed stale docs, artifacts, and the old website package.

sections.title: Navigation
items: Added Home and Files keyboard shortcuts and cleaned up sidebar naming, ordering, and record-relation filters.

weeks.label: Week of Apr 17 through Apr 24
weeks.summary: Production calling stabilized around queue failure handling and Redis isolation while workspace tasks became a publishable flow with deploy, browser, logs, and review automation.

sections.title: Dialer
items: Made post-call analysis Pi-native and wired live coaching hooks with analysis persistence.
Recovered stale voice preflight locks, aligned hosted Twilio caller-id fallback behavior, and instrumented preflight failures.
Stopped queue auto-dial retry loops, gated dialing on Twilio device readiness, persisted local preflight failures, and surfaced browser call failures before ringing.
Preserved predictive queue parallel handoff, normalized predictive queue numbers, restored raw-number fallbacks, and unblocked queue-runner handoff.

sections.title: Redis and API Reliability
items: Centralized Redis client lifecycle, isolated queue connections, kept pubsub clients alive, and deduped repeated Redis Sentry captures.
Fixed hosted Twilio settings nullability, lane typing, and queue-selection behavior when dialer config columns are missing.

sections.title: Workspace Agents
items: Added task and stream workflow scripts for push, PR, review, merge, root aliases, safer task starts, and automatic workpad creation.
Migrated task metadata into .task, saved workpads during task push, and added Supabase-backed stream context and context lookup previews.
Added browser automation, Railway logs with boot-noise filtering, deployment watch helpers, and a Cloudflare Pages website deploy script.
Rewrote workflow documentation around publish flow, TMP handling, task lifecycle, link formatting, memory use, and coding standards.

sections.title: Website
items: Removed the legacy website v1 package and unused feed image assets.

weeks.label: Week of Apr 24 through May 1
weeks.summary: The month closed with the workspace facade and decision engine becoming the main agent substrate, plus fixes for parallel-dialer failure modes and CSV import naming.

sections.title: Workspace Agents
items: Added a typed workspace facade with standard envelopes, top-level HTTP and Linear tools, browser aliases, filesystem helpers, task commands, doctor/status commands, and PR/review helpers.
Added an evidence decision system with belief and confidence ranking, token estimates, LangSmith trace grouping, OpenRouter Qwen embeddings with local fallback, and AI code review confidence.
Hardened publish workflow behavior with task pin persistence, current.json writes, metadata conflict resolution, stream sync push selection, root publish commands, and a verify safety gate.
Improved Railway and deployment tooling with active deployment targeting, network and HTTP logs, commit metadata, deploy watch behavior, and portable agent scripts.

sections.title: Dialer
items: Fixed parallel caller-id self-locks, conflict loops, result typing, provider/autostart errors, active queue start loops, queue state drift, phone validation, and create-failure surfacing.
Added the CSV import naming modal and resolved review findings around import naming behavior.
Preserved double-dial fallback attempts and released parallel caller-id locks when the winning call completes.

sections.title: Docs and Steering
items: Expanded agent steering for mandatory bootstrap, Codex and MCP setup, sub-agent usage, shell-command construction, link formatting, exploration, and documentation habits.
Added token-file gitignore coverage and stripped internal runtime noise from facade envelopes, logs, and docs.

sections.title: Infrastructure
items: Kept local index dependencies out of Railway, downgraded tree-sitter for Node 24 compatibility, and tightened tailnet-only auth behavior for local agents.
